questions.prompt: A company collects data for temperature, humidity, and atmospheric pressure in cities across multiple continents. The average volume of data that the company collects from each site daily is 500 GB. Each site has a high-speed Internet connection. The company wants to aggregate the data from all these global sites as quickly as possible in a single Amazon S3 bucket. The solution must minimize operational complexity. Which solution meets these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Turn on S3 Transfer Acceleration on the destination S3 bucket and use multipart uploads to directly upload site data to the destination S3 bucket.
questions.explanation: S3 Transfer Acceleration uses Amazon CloudFront edge locations to accelerate uploads over long distances and can significantly speed transfers. Multipart uploads allow large objects to be uploaded efficiently and in parallel, minimizing time and operational complexity.

questions.prompt: A company needs the ability to analyze the log files of its proprietary application. The logs are stored in JSON format in an Amazon S3 bucket. Queries will be simple and will run on-demand. A solutions architect needs to perform the analysis with minimal changes to the existing architecture. What should the solutions architect do to meet these requirements with the LEAST amount of operational overhead?
questions.correctAnswer: Use Amazon Athena directly with Amazon S3 to run the queries as needed.
questions.explanation: Amazon Athena lets you run SQL queries directly against data stored in S3 with no infrastructure to manage, making it ideal for ad-hoc analysis of JSON logs with minimal operational overhead.

questions.prompt: A company uses AWS Organizations to manage multiple AWS accounts for different departments. The management account has an Amazon S3 bucket that contains project reports. The company wants to limit access to this S3 bucket to only users of accounts within the organization in AWS Organizations. Which solution meets these requirements with the LEAST amount of operational overhead?
questions.correctAnswer: Add the aws:PrincipalOrgID global condition key with a reference to the organization ID to the S3 bucket policy.
questions.explanation: The aws:PrincipalOrgID condition key lets you restrict access in a resource-based policy to principals that belong to a specified AWS Organization, avoiding the need to list all account IDs.

questions.prompt: An application runs on an Amazon EC2 instance in a VPC. The application processes logs that are stored in an Amazon S3 bucket. The EC2 instance needs to access the S3 bucket without connectivity to the internet. Which solution will provide private network connectivity to Amazon S3?
questions.correctAnswer: Create a gateway VPC endpoint to the S3 bucket.
questions.explanation: A gateway VPC endpoint enables private connectivity from VPC resources to Amazon S3 without requiring an internet gateway, NAT device, or public IP addresses.

questions.prompt: A company is hosting a web application on AWS using a single Amazon EC2 instance that stores user-uploaded documents in an Amazon EBS volume. For better scalability and availability, the company duplicated the architecture and created a second EC2 instance and EBS volume in another Availability Zone, placing both behind an Application Load Balancer. After completing this change, users reported that, each time they refreshed the website, they could see one subset of their documents or the other, but never all of the documents at the same time. What should a solutions architect propose to ensure users see all of their documents at once?
questions.correctAnswer: Copy the data from both EBS volumes to Amazon EFS. Modify the application to save new documents to Amazon EFS.
questions.explanation: Amazon EFS provides a shared, highly available file system that can be mounted by multiple EC2 instances across Availability Zones, ensuring all instances see the same files in real time.

questions.prompt: A company uses NFS to store large video files in on-premises network attached storage. Each video file ranges in size from 1 MB to 500 GB. The total storage is 70 TB and is no longer growing. The company decides to migrate the video files to Amazon S3. The company must migrate the video files as soon as possible while using the least possible network bandwidth. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Use AWS Snowball Edge to physically transfer the 70 TB to AWS (export the data to Snowball Edge, ship it to AWS, and have AWS import into S3).
questions.explanation: Snowball Edge moves large amounts of data without using network bandwidth. Although device delivery and import take several days, the data transfer itself to the device is very fast and uses no internet bandwidth.

questions.prompt: A company has an application that ingests incoming messages. Dozens of other applications and microservices then quickly consume these messages. The number of messages varies drastically and sometimes increases suddenly to 100,000 each second. The company wants to decouple the solution and increase scalability. Which solution meets these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Publish the messages to an Amazon SNS topic with multiple Amazon SQS subscriptions. Configure the consumer applications to process the messages from the queues.
questions.explanation: Using SNS with multiple SQS subscriptions decouples producers from consumers, enables fan-out, and lets each consumer scale independently. SQS provides buffering and can be combined with filtering policies so each queue only receives relevant messages.

questions.prompt: A company is migrating a distributed application to AWS. The application serves variable workloads. The legacy platform consists of a primary server that coordinates jobs across multiple compute nodes. The company wants to modernize the application with a solution that maximizes resiliency and scalability. How should a solutions architect design the architecture to meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Configure an Amazon SQS queue as a destination for the jobs. Implement the compute nodes with Amazon EC2 instances that are managed in an Auto Scaling group. Configure EC2 Auto Scaling based on the size of the queue.
questions.explanation: Using SQS decouples job producers from consumers. Auto Scaling based on queue metrics allows compute capacity to scale with backlog, improving resiliency and scalability.

questions.prompt: A company is running an SMB file server in its data center. The file server stores large files that are accessed frequently for the first few days after the files are created. After 7 days the files are rarely accessed. The total data size is increasing and is close to the company's total storage capacity. A solutions architect must increase the company's available storage space without losing low-latency access to the most recently accessed files. The solutions architect must also provide file lifecycle management to avoid future storage issues. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Create an Amazon S3 File Gateway to extend the company's storage space. Create an S3 Lifecycle policy to transition the data to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 7 days.
questions.explanation: S3 File Gateway presents an NFS/SMB file share that stores objects in S3, extending on-premises storage to S3. Lifecycle policies can transition older objects to low-cost archive storage.

questions.prompt: A company is building an ecommerce web application on AWS. The application sends information about new orders to an Amazon API Gateway REST API to process. The company wants to ensure that orders are processed in the order that they are received. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Use an API Gateway integration to send a message to an Amazon SQS FIFO queue when the application receives an order. Configure the SQS FIFO queue to invoke an AWS Lambda function for processing.
questions.explanation: SQS FIFO queues preserve message order and exactly-once processing semantics. Using a FIFO queue guarantees that orders are processed in order, and Lambda can be invoked to process them.

questions.prompt: A company has an application that runs on Amazon EC2 instances and uses an Amazon Aurora database. The EC2 instances connect to the database by using user names and passwords that are stored locally in a file. The company wants to minimize the operational overhead of credential management. What should a solutions architect do to accomplish this goal?
questions.correctAnswer: Use AWS Secrets Manager and attach an IAM role that grants access to that secret to the EC2 instances that need it. Turn on automatic rotation.
questions.explanation: AWS Secrets Manager centralizes secret storage, can automatically rotate database credentials, and IAM roles let EC2 instances retrieve secrets securely without embedding credentials in files.

questions.prompt: A global company hosts its web application on Amazon EC2 instances behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB). The web application has static data and dynamic data. The company stores its static data in an Amazon S3 bucket. The company wants to improve performance and reduce latency for the static data and dynamic data. The company is using its own domain name registered with Amazon Route 53. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Create an Amazon CloudFront distribution that has the S3 bucket and the ALB as origins. Configure Route 53 to route traffic to the CloudFront distribution.
questions.explanation: CloudFront can cache static content from S3 and accelerate dynamic content from the ALB by using multiple origins. Using Route 53 with CloudFront allows the company domain to route to the distribution and reduce latency globally.

questions.prompt: A company performs monthly maintenance on its AWS infrastructure. During these maintenance activities, the company needs to rotate the credentials for its Amazon RDS for MySQL databases across multiple AWS Regions. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
questions.correctAnswer: Store the credentials as secrets in AWS Secrets Manager. Use multi-Region secret replication for the required Regions. Configure Secrets Manager to rotate the secrets on a schedule.
questions.explanation: Secrets Manager supports scheduled rotation and multi-Region replication to keep secrets available in multiple Regions with minimal operational effort.

questions.prompt: A company runs an ecommerce application on Amazon EC2 instances behind an Application Load Balancer. The instances run in an Amazon EC2 Auto Scaling group across multiple Availability Zones. The Auto Scaling group scales based on CPU utilization metrics. The ecommerce application stores the transaction data in a MySQL 8.0 database that is hosted on a large EC2 instance. The database's performance degrades quickly as application load increases. The application handles more read requests than write transactions. The company wants a solution that will automatically scale the database to meet the demand of unpredictable read workloads while maintaining high availability. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Use Amazon Aurora with a Multi-AZ deployment. Configure Aurora Auto Scaling with Aurora Replicas.
questions.explanation: Amazon Aurora provides high performance and replication across AZs. Aurora Auto Scaling can add or remove Aurora Replicas automatically to handle read scaling while preserving high availability.

questions.prompt: A company recently migrated to AWS and wants to implement a solution to protect the traffic that flows in and out of the production VPC. The company had an inspection server in its on-premises data center. The inspection server performed specific operations such as traffic flow inspection and traffic filtering. The company wants to have the same functionalities in the AWS Cloud. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Use AWS Network Firewall to create the required rules for traffic inspection and traffic filtering for the production VPC.
questions.explanation: AWS Network Firewall is a managed service that provides network traffic inspection and filtering capabilities for VPCs and supports rule-based filtering similar to on-premises inspection appliances.

questions.prompt: A company hosts a data lake on AWS. The data lake consists of data in Amazon S3 and Amazon RDS for PostgreSQL. The company needs a reporting solution that provides data visualization and includes all the data sources within the data lake. Only the company's management team should have full access to all the visualizations. The rest of the company should have only limited access. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Create an analysis in Amazon QuickSight. Connect all the data sources and create new datasets. Publish dashboards to visualize the data. Share the dashboards with the appropriate users and groups.
questions.explanation: Amazon QuickSight connects to multiple data sources, creates analyses and dashboards, and supports fine-grained sharing and access controls to restrict dashboard access based on user/group permissions.

questions.prompt: A company is implementing a new business application. The application runs on two Amazon EC2 instances and uses an Amazon S3 bucket for document storage. A solutions architect needs to ensure that the EC2 instances can access the S3 bucket. What should the solutions architect do to meet this requirement?
questions.correctAnswer: Create an IAM role that grants access to the S3 bucket. Attach the role to the EC2 instances.
questions.explanation: Attaching an IAM role with appropriate permissions to EC2 instances enables the instances to access S3 without storing long-lived credentials.

questions.prompt: An application development team is designing a microservice that will convert large images to smaller, compressed images. When a user uploads an image through the web interface, the microservice should store the image in an Amazon S3 bucket, process and compress the image with an AWS Lambda function, and store the image in its compressed form in a different S3 bucket. A solutions architect needs to design a solution that uses durable, stateless components to process the images automatically. Which combination of actions will meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
correctAnswer: Create an Amazon SQS queue. Configure the S3 bucket to send a notification to the SQS queue when an image is uploaded to the S3 bucket.
Configure the Lambda function to use the Amazon SQS queue as the invocation source. When the SQS message is successfully processed, delete the message in the queue.
questions.explanation: Using SQS provides durable buffering for S3 upload notifications and decouples uploads from processing. Lambda can poll SQS as an event source to process messages reliably and delete them on success.

questions.prompt: A company has a three-tier web application that is deployed on AWS. The web servers are deployed in a public subnet in a VPC. The application servers and database servers are deployed in private subnets in the same VPC. The company has deployed a third-party virtual firewall appliance from AWS Marketplace in an inspection VPC. The appliance is configured with an IP interface that can accept IP packets. A solutions architect needs to integrate the web application with the appliance to inspect all traffic to the application before the traffic reaches the web server. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
questions.correctAnswer: Use Gateway Load Balancer and Gateway Load Balancer endpoints to steer traffic to the inspection VPC appliances.
questions.explanation: Gateway Load Balancer (GWLB) simplifies deploying, scaling, and managing virtual appliances for inline traffic inspection. GWLB endpoints let spoke VPCs send traffic to appliances in an inspection VPC with minimal operational overhead.

questions.prompt: A company wants to improve its ability to clone large amounts of production data into a test environment in the same AWS Region. The data is stored in Amazon EC2 instances on Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volumes. Modifications to the cloned data must not affect the production environment. The software that accesses this data requires consistently high I/O performance. A solutions architect needs to minimize the time that is required to clone the production data into the test environment. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Use Amazon EBS snapshots with Fast Snapshot Restore (FSR) to create new volumes that are fully initialized at creation, then attach the volumes to test instances.
questions.explanation: EBS Fast Snapshot Restore provides volumes that are pre-initialized so they deliver full performance immediately after creation, minimizing time-to-clone while keeping production and test data isolated.

questions.prompt: An ecommerce company wants to launch a one-deal-a-day website on AWS. Each day will feature exactly one product on sale for a period of 24 hours. The company wants to be able to handle millions of requests each hour with millisecond latency during peak hours. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
questions.correctAnswer: Host the website static content in an Amazon S3 bucket with CloudFront distribution; use Amazon API Gateway and AWS Lambda for backend APIs; store data in Amazon DynamoDB.
questions.explanation: S3 + CloudFront serve static content at scale with low latency. API Gateway + Lambda provide fully managed, scalable backend APIs. DynamoDB provides single-digit millisecond latency at scale with minimal operations.

questions.prompt: A solutions architect is using Amazon S3 to design the storage architecture of a new digital media application. The media files must be resilient to the loss of an Availability Zone. Some files are accessed frequently while other files are rarely accessed in an unpredictable pattern. The solutions architect must minimize the costs of storing and retrieving the media files. Which storage option meets these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: S3 Intelligent-Tiering
questions.explanation: S3 Intelligent-Tiering automatically moves objects between access tiers based on usage patterns to minimize cost while remaining durable and AZ-resilient (S3 stores objects across AZs within a Region).

questions.prompt: A company is storing backup files by using Amazon S3 Standard storage. The files are accessed frequently for 1 month. However, the files are not accessed after 1 month. The company must keep the files indefinitely. Which storage solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
questions.correctAnswer: Create an S3 Lifecycle configuration to transition objects from S3 Standard to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 month.
questions.explanation: S3 Glacier Deep Archive is the lowest-cost storage class for long-term retention when occasional hours-long retrieval is acceptable; lifecycle policies automate the transition after 1 month.

questions.prompt: A company observes an increase in Amazon EC2 costs in its most recent bill. The billing team notices unwanted vertical scaling of instance types for a couple of EC2 instances. A solutions architect needs to create a graph comparing the last 2 months of EC2 costs and perform an in-depth analysis to identify the root cause of the vertical scaling. How should the solutions architect generate the information with the LEAST operational overhead?
questions.correctAnswer: Use Cost Explorer's granular filtering feature to perform an in-depth analysis of EC2 costs based on instance types.
questions.explanation: AWS Cost Explorer provides built-in filtering and visualization to analyze cost trends by resource type like EC2 instance types with minimal setup.

questions.prompt: A company is designing an application. The application uses an AWS Lambda function to receive information through Amazon API Gateway and to store the information in an Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL database. During the proof-of-concept stage, the company has to increase the Lambda quotas significantly to handle the high volumes of data that the company needs to load into the database. A solutions architect must recommend a new design to improve scalability and minimize the configuration effort. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Set up two Lambda functions: one to receive the information and one to load the information into the database. Integrate the Lambda functions by using an Amazon SQS queue.
questions.explanation: Separating ingestion from loading and using SQS decouples the workflow and smooths variable loads; each function can scale independently without complex quota changes.

questions.prompt: A company needs to review its AWS Cloud deployment to ensure that its Amazon S3 buckets do not have unauthorized configuration changes. What should a solutions architect do to accomplish this goal?
questions.correctAnswer: Turn on AWS Config with the appropriate rules.
questions.explanation: AWS Config records resource configurations and supports rules to detect unauthorized changes to S3 buckets and other resources.

questions.prompt: A company is launching a new application and will display application metrics on an Amazon CloudWatch dashboard. The company's product manager needs to access this dashboard periodically. The product manager does not have an AWS account. A solutions architect must provide access to the product manager by following the principle of least privilege. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Share the dashboard from the CloudWatch console by entering the product manager's email address and provide a shareable link.
questions.explanation: CloudWatch dashboards can be shared with specific email addresses; viewers create a password to view the dashboard, avoiding full AWS accounts for guest viewers.

questions.prompt: A company is migrating applications to AWS. The applications are deployed in different accounts. The company manages the accounts centrally by using AWS Organizations. The company's security team needs a single sign-on (SSO) solution across all the company's accounts. The company must continue managing the users and groups in its on-premises self-managed Microsoft Active Directory. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Enable AWS Single Sign-On (AWS SSO) and configure a one-way forest or domain trust between the on-premises Active Directory and AWS SSO using AWS Directory Service for Microsoft AD.
questions.explanation: AWS SSO (now IAM Identity Center) can integrate with an on-premises AD via a trust to provide centralized SSO across AWS accounts while keeping user and group management in the on-premises AD.

questions.prompt: A company provides a Voice over Internet Protocol (VoIP) service that uses UDP connections. The service consists of Amazon EC2 instances that run in an Auto Scaling group. The company has deployments across multiple AWS Regions. The company needs to route users to the Region with the lowest latency. The company also needs automated failover between Regions. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Deploy a Network Load Balancer (NLB) and an associated target group in each Region, attach the Auto Scaling group, and use AWS Global Accelerator with the NLB as endpoints to route users to the lowest-latency Region with automated failover.
questions.explanation: Global Accelerator provides global traffic management and routes traffic to the optimal Region based on health and latency, and it works with NLB endpoints that can handle UDP traffic required by VoIP.

questions.prompt: A development team runs monthly resource-intensive tests on its general purpose Amazon RDS for MySQL DB instance with Performance Insights enabled. The testing lasts for 48 hours once a month and is the only process that uses the database. The team wants to reduce the cost of running the tests without reducing the compute and memory attributes of the DB instance. Which solution meets these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
questions.correctAnswer: Create a snapshot when tests are completed. Terminate the DB instance and restore the snapshot when required.
questions.explanation: Taking a snapshot and terminating the instance saves compute costs; restoring the snapshot recreates the instance with the same configuration when testing is needed.

questions.prompt: A company that hosts its web application on AWS wants to ensure all Amazon EC2 instances, Amazon RDS DB instances, and Amazon Redshift clusters are configured with tags. The company wants to minimize the effort of configuring and operating this check. What should a solutions architect do to accomplish this?
questions.correctAnswer: Use AWS Config rules to define and detect resources that are not properly tagged.
questions.explanation: AWS Config rules can evaluate tagging compliance and provide a centralized, automated way to detect untagged or mis-tagged resources.

questions.prompt: A development team needs to host a website that will be accessed by other teams. The website contents consist of HTML, CSS, client-side JavaScript, and images. Which method is the MOST cost-effective for hosting the website?
questions.correctAnswer: Create an Amazon S3 bucket and host the website there.
questions.explanation: S3 static website hosting is simple, highly available, and cost-effective for static content like HTML, CSS, JS, and images.

questions.prompt: A company runs an online marketplace web application on AWS. The application serves hundreds of thousands of users during peak hours. The company needs a scalable, near-real-time solution to share the details of millions of financial transactions with several other internal applications. Transactions also need to be processed to remove sensitive data before being stored in a document database for low-latency retrieval. What should a solutions architect recommend to meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Stream the transactions into Amazon Kinesis Data Streams. Use AWS Lambda to remove sensitive data from every transaction and then store the transactions data in Amazon DynamoDB. Other applications can consume the transactions data off the Kinesis data stream.
questions.explanation: Kinesis Data Streams supports real-time ingestion at massive scale. Lambda can process and redact sensitive fields before storing the results in DynamoDB for low-latency access; other consumers can read from the stream as needed.

questions.prompt: A company hosts its multi-tier applications on AWS. For compliance, governance, auditing, and security, the company must track configuration changes on its AWS resources and record a history of API calls made to these resources. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Use AWS Config to track configuration changes and AWS CloudTrail to record API calls.
questions.explanation: AWS Config provides configuration monitoring and history; CloudTrail records API activity. Together they meet compliance and auditing requirements.

questions.prompt: A company is preparing to launch a public-facing web application in the AWS Cloud. The architecture consists of Amazon EC2 instances within a VPC behind an Elastic Load Balancer (ELB). A third-party service is used for the DNS. The company's solutions architect must recommend a solution to detect and protect against large-scale DDoS attacks. Which solution meets these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Enable AWS Shield Advanced and assign the ELB to it.
questions.explanation: AWS Shield Advanced provides expanded DDoS attack protection for your Amazon EC2 instances, Elastic Load Balancing load balancers, CloudFront distributions, Route 53 hosted zones, and AWS Global Accelerator standard accelerators.

questions.prompt: A company recently launched a variety of new workloads on Amazon EC2 instances in its AWS account. The company needs to create a strategy to access and administer the instances remotely and securely. The company needs to implement a repeatable process that works with native AWS services and follows the AWS Well-Architected Framework. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
questions.correctAnswer: Attach the appropriate IAM role to each existing instance and new instance. Use AWS Systems Manager Session Manager to establish a remote SSH session.
questions.explanation: Session Manager provides secure, auditable remote access without opening inbound ports or managing SSH keys, and attaching IAM roles allows instances to use SSM features.

questions.prompt: A company is hosting a static website on Amazon S3 and is using Amazon Route 53 for DNS. The website is experiencing increased demand from around the world. The company must decrease latency for users who access the website. Which solution meets these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
questions.correctAnswer: Add an Amazon CloudFront distribution in front of the S3 bucket and update Route 53 records to point to the CloudFront distribution.
questions.explanation: CloudFront caches content at edge locations globally, reducing latency for geographically distributed users while remaining cost-effective for static websites.

questions.prompt: A company maintains a searchable repository of items on its website. The data is stored in an Amazon RDS for MySQL database table that contains more than 10 million rows. The database has 2 TB of General Purpose SSD storage. There are millions of updates against this data every day through the company's website. The company has noticed that some insert operations are taking 10 seconds or longer. The company has determined that the database storage performance is the problem. Which solution addresses this performance issue?
questions.correctAnswer: Change the storage type to Provisioned IOPS (io1/io2) SSD.
questions.explanation: Provisioned IOPS SSD storage provides consistently high I/O performance and throughput, which can improve the latency of insert operations for write-heavy workloads.

questions.prompt: A company has thousands of edge devices that collectively generate 1 TB of status alerts each day. Each alert is approximately 2 KB in size. A solutions architect needs to implement a solution to ingest and store the alerts for future analysis. The company wants a highly available solution. However, the company needs to minimize costs and does not want to manage additional infrastructure. Additionally, the company wants to keep 14 days of data available for immediate analysis and archive any data older than 14 days. What is the MOST operationally efficient solution that meets these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Create an Amazon Kinesis Data Firehose delivery stream to ingest the alerts and deliver them to an Amazon S3 bucket. Configure an S3 Lifecycle rule to transition data to S3 Glacier after 14 days.
questions.explanation: Kinesis Data Firehose is fully managed for ingestion and can deliver to S3. S3 lifecycle policies automate archival after 14 days; this avoids managing infrastructure and keeps recent data readily available.

questions.prompt: A company's application integrates with multiple software-as-a-service (SaaS) sources for data collection. The company runs Amazon EC2 instances to receive the data and to upload the data to an Amazon S3 bucket for analysis. The same EC2 instance that receives and uploads the data also sends a notification to the user when an upload is complete. The company has noticed slow application performance and wants to improve the performance as much as possible. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
questions.correctAnswer: Create an Amazon AppFlow flow to transfer data between each SaaS source and the S3 bucket. Configure an S3 event notification to send events to an Amazon SNS topic when the upload to the S3 bucket is complete.
questions.explanation: AppFlow is a managed integration service that can ingest data directly from SaaS apps to S3 without using EC2 as a middleman. S3 event notifications to SNS can trigger notifications when uploads complete, offloading work from EC2.

questions.prompt: A company runs a highly available image-processing application on Amazon EC2 instances in a single VPC. The EC2 instances run inside several subnets across multiple Availability Zones. The EC2 instances do not communicate with each other. However, the EC2 instances download images from Amazon S3 and upload images to Amazon S3 through a single NAT gateway. The company is concerned about data transfer charges. What is the MOST cost-effective way for the company to avoid Regional data transfer charges?
questions.correctAnswer: Deploy a gateway VPC endpoint for Amazon S3.
questions.explanation: Using a gateway VPC endpoint for S3 keeps S3 traffic within the AWS network and avoids data transfer charges associated with routing through a NAT gateway.

questions.prompt: A company has an on-premises application that generates a large amount of time-sensitive data that is backed up to Amazon S3. The application has grown and there are user complaints about internet bandwidth limitations. A solutions architect needs to design a long-term solution that allows for both timely backups to Amazon S3 and with minimal impact on internet connectivity for internal users. Which solution meets these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Establish a new AWS Direct Connect connection and direct backup traffic through this new connection.
questions.explanation: AWS Direct Connect provides a dedicated network link between on-premises and AWS that bypasses the public internet, providing more consistent bandwidth and reducing impact on internet connectivity for other users.

questions.prompt: A company has an Amazon S3 bucket that contains critical data. The company must protect the data from accidental deletion. Which combination of steps should a solutions architect take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
correctAnswer: Enable versioning on the S3 bucket
Enable MFA Delete on the S3 bucket
questions.explanation: Versioning keeps previous object versions so deleted objects can be restored. MFA Delete adds an extra authentication requirement for destructive changes, reducing the risk of accidental deletions.

questions.prompt: A company has a data ingestion workflow that consists of: an Amazon SNS topic for notifications about new data deliveries and an AWS Lambda function to process the data and record metadata. The company observes that the ingestion workflow fails occasionally because of network connectivity issues. When such a failure occurs, the Lambda function does not ingest the corresponding data unless the company manually reruns the job. Which combination of actions should a solutions architect take to ensure that the Lambda function ingests all data in the future? (Choose two.)
correctAnswer: Create an Amazon SQS queue, and subscribe it to the SNS topic
Modify the Lambda function to read from the Amazon SQS queue
questions.explanation: Subscribing an SQS queue to SNS provides durable storage of messages when downstream processing fails. Having Lambda read from SQS decouples processing and allows retries and visibility into failed messages.

questions.prompt: A company has an application that provides marketing services to stores. The stores upload transaction data to the company through SFTP, and the data is processed and analyzed to generate new marketing offers. Some of the files can exceed 200 GB in size. Recently, the company discovered that some of the stores have uploaded files that contain personally identifiable information (PII) that should not have been included. The company wants administrators to be alerted if PII is shared again. The company also wants to automate remediation. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements with the LEAST development effort?
questions.correctAnswer: Use an Amazon S3 bucket as a secure transfer point. Use Amazon Macie to scan the objects in the bucket. If objects contain PII, use Amazon SNS to notify administrators and trigger remediation workflows.
questions.explanation: Amazon Macie can detect PII in S3 objects; integrating with SNS enables alerting and can trigger automated remediation steps with minimal custom development.

questions.prompt: A company needs guaranteed Amazon EC2 capacity in three specific Availability Zones in a specific AWS Region for an upcoming event that will last 1 week. What should the company do to guarantee the EC2 capacity?
questions.correctAnswer: Create an On-Demand Capacity Reservation that specifies the Region and the three Availability Zones needed.
questions.explanation: On-Demand Capacity Reservations reserve EC2 capacity in specified AZs for a given period, guaranteeing capacity without long-term commitment.

questions.prompt: A company's website uses an Amazon EC2 instance store for its catalog of items. The company wants to make sure that the catalog is highly available and that the catalog is stored in a durable location. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Move the catalog to an Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) file system.
questions.explanation: EFS is a managed, durable, and highly available file system that can be mounted by multiple instances, replacing ephemeral instance store for durable shared storage.

questions.prompt: A company stores call transcript files on a monthly basis. Users access the files randomly within 1 year of the call, but users access the files infrequently after 1 year. The company wants to optimize its solution by giving users the ability to query and retrieve files that are less than 1-year-old as quickly as possible. A delay in retrieving older files is acceptable. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
questions.correctAnswer: Store files in S3 Intelligent-Tiering and set an S3 Lifecycle policy to move files to S3 Glacier Flexible Retrieval after 1 year. Use Amazon Athena to query files in S3 and S3 Glacier Select to retrieve older files as needed.
questions.explanation: Intelligent-Tiering reduces storage costs for mixed access patterns. Athena allows fast queries on S3 objects for recent data; Glacier Select can query or retrieve archived data when needed.

questions.prompt: A company has a production workload that runs on 1,000 Amazon EC2 Linux instances. The workload is powered by third-party software. The company needs to patch the third-party software on all EC2 instances as quickly as possible to remediate a critical security vulnerability. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Use AWS Systems Manager Run Command to run a custom command that applies the patch to all EC2 instances.
questions.explanation: Run Command can execute scripts or commands across many instances quickly and securely without managing SSH access or custom tooling.

questions.prompt: A company is developing an application that provides order shipping statistics for retrieval by a REST API. The company wants to extract the shipping statistics, organize the data into an easy-to-read HTML format, and send the report to several email addresses at the same time every morning. Which combination of steps should a solutions architect take to meet these requirements? (Choose two.)
correctAnswer: Create an Amazon EventBridge scheduled event that invokes an AWS Lambda function to query the application's API for the data
Use Amazon Simple Email Service (SES) to format the data and to send the report by email
questions.explanation: EventBridge can schedule daily invocation of a Lambda function to gather and format data, and SES can send formatted emails to multiple recipients.

questions.prompt: A company wants to migrate its on-premises application to AWS. The application produces output files that vary in size from tens of gigabytes to hundreds of terabytes. The application data must be stored in a standard file system structure. The company wants a solution that scales automatically, is highly available, and requires minimum operational overhead. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Migrate the application to Amazon EC2 instances in a Multi-AZ Auto Scaling group and use Amazon EFS for storage.
questions.explanation: EFS provides a managed, scalable network file system that supports POSIX semantics and scales automatically; combined with Auto Scaling EC2 instances it meets the requirements.

questions.prompt: A company needs to store its accounting records in Amazon S3. The records must be immediately accessible for 1 year and then must be archived for an additional 9 years. No one at the company, including administrative users and root users, can be able to delete the records during the entire 10-year period. The records must be stored with maximum resiliency. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Use an S3 Lifecycle policy to transition the records from S3 Standard to S3 Glacier Deep Archive after 1 year, and enable S3 Object Lock in Compliance mode for a retention period of 10 years.
questions.explanation: Object Lock in Compliance mode prevents deletion for the retention period (including by root or admins). Glacier Deep Archive provides the lowest-cost long-term storage for the final 9 years.

questions.prompt: A company runs multiple Windows workloads on AWS. The company's employees use Windows file shares that are hosted on two Amazon EC2 instances. The file shares synchronize data between themselves and maintain duplicate copies. The company wants a highly available and durable storage solution that preserves how users currently access the files. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Extend the file share environment to Amazon FSx for Windows File Server with a Multi-AZ configuration and migrate all the data to FSx.
questions.explanation: FSx for Windows File Server provides a managed, highly available native Windows file system with SMB access and supports features like DFS and user experience similar to Windows file shares.

questions.prompt: A solutions architect is developing a VPC architecture that includes multiple subnets. The architecture will host applications that use Amazon EC2 instances and Amazon RDS DB instances. The architecture consists of six subnets in two Availability Zones. Each Availability Zone includes a public subnet, a private subnet, and a dedicated subnet for databases. Only EC2 instances that run in the private subnets can have access to the RDS databases. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Create a security group that allows inbound traffic from the security group that is assigned to instances in the private subnets. Attach the security group to the DB instances.
questions.explanation: Using security groups as sources allows DB instances to accept traffic only from EC2 instances that have the matching security group, restricting access to instances in private subnets without relying on IP addresses.

questions.prompt: A company has registered its domain name with Amazon Route 53. The company uses Amazon API Gateway in the ca-central-1 Region as a public interface for its backend microservice APIs. Third-party services consume the APIs securely. The company wants to design its API Gateway URL with the company's domain name and corresponding certificate so that the third-party services can use HTTPS. Which solution will meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Create a Regional API Gateway endpoint. Associate the API Gateway endpoint with the company's domain name. Import the public certificate associated with the company's domain name into AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) in the same Region and attach the certificate to the API Gateway endpoint. Configure Route 53 to route traffic to the API Gateway endpoint.
questions.explanation: Regional API Gateway endpoints can use certificates from ACM in the same Region and be mapped to custom domain names; Route 53 can route the domain to the API Gateway.

questions.prompt: A company is running a popular social media website. The website gives users the ability to upload images to share with other users. The company wants to make sure that the images do not contain inappropriate content. The company needs a solution that minimizes development effort. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Use Amazon Rekognition to detect inappropriate content and use human review for low-confidence predictions.
questions.explanation: Rekognition provides image moderation capabilities that can flag inappropriate content; human review can be used to validate low-confidence cases.

questions.prompt: A company wants to run its critical applications in containers to meet requirements for scalability and availability. The company prefers to focus on maintenance of the critical applications. The company does not want to be responsible for provisioning and managing the underlying infrastructure that runs the containerized workload. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Use Amazon ECS on AWS Fargate.
questions.explanation: ECS on Fargate is a serverless compute option for containers that removes the need to manage EC2 instances while providing scaling and availability.

questions.prompt: A company hosts more than 300 global websites and applications. The company requires a platform to analyze more than 30 TB of clickstream data each day. What should a solutions architect do to transmit and process the clickstream data?
questions.correctAnswer: Collect the data with Amazon Kinesis Data Streams, use Kinesis Data Firehose to deliver the data to an S3 data lake, and load the data into Amazon Redshift for analysis.
questions.explanation: Kinesis Data Streams supports high-throughput ingestion at scale; Firehose can reliably deliver data to S3; Redshift can perform analytics on large datasets.

questions.prompt: A company has a website hosted on AWS. The website is behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB) that is configured to handle HTTP and HTTPS separately. The company wants to forward all requests to the website so that the requests will use HTTPS. What should a solutions architect do to meet this requirement?
questions.correctAnswer: Create a listener rule on the ALB to redirect HTTP traffic to HTTPS.
questions.explanation: ALB listener rules can redirect requests on port 80 to port 443, enforcing HTTPS for incoming requests.

questions.prompt: A company is developing a two-tier web application on AWS. The company's developers have deployed the application on an Amazon EC2 instance that connects directly to a backend Amazon RDS database. The company must not hardcode database credentials in the application. The company must also implement a solution to automatically rotate the database credentials on a regular basis. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
questions.correctAnswer: Store the database credentials as a secret in AWS Secrets Manager. Turn on automatic rotation for the secret. Attach the required permission to the EC2 role to grant access to the secret.
questions.explanation: Secrets Manager centralizes credential storage and supports automatic rotation. Granting the EC2 instance role permission to access the secret removes hardcoded credentials.

questions.prompt: A company is deploying a new public web application to AWS. The application will run behind an Application Load Balancer (ALB). The application needs to be encrypted at the edge with an SSL/TLS certificate that is issued by an external certificate authority (CA). The certificate must be rotated each year before the certificate expires. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Use AWS Certificate Manager (ACM) to import the SSL/TLS certificate and apply it to the ALB. Use Amazon EventBridge to send a notification when the certificate is nearing expiration and rotate the certificate manually.
questions.explanation: ACM supports importing third-party certificates for use with ALB. EventBridge can schedule notifications for certificate expiry; manual rotation is required for imported third-party certificates.

questions.prompt: A company runs its infrastructure on AWS and has a registered base of 700,000 users for its document management application. The company intends to create a product that converts large .pdf files to .jpg image files. The .pdf files average 5 MB in size. The company needs to store the original files and the converted files. A solutions architect must design a scalable solution to accommodate demand that will grow rapidly over time. Which solution meets these requirements MOST cost-effectively?
questions.correctAnswer: Save the .pdf files to Amazon S3. Configure an S3 PUT event to invoke an AWS Lambda function to convert the files to .jpg format and store them back in Amazon S3.
questions.explanation: S3 + Lambda provides an event-driven, serverless, and cost-effective way to process files at scale without managing servers.

questions.prompt: A company has more than 5 TB of file data on Windows file servers that run on premises. Users and applications interact with the data each day. The company is moving its Windows workloads to AWS. As the company continues this process, the company requires access to AWS and on-premises file storage with minimum latency. The company needs a solution that minimizes operational overhead and requires no significant changes to the existing file access patterns. The company uses an AWS Site-to-Site VPN connection for connectivity to AWS. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?
questions.correctAnswer: Deploy and configure Amazon FSx for Windows File Server on AWS and deploy and configure an Amazon FSx File Gateway on premises. Move the on-premises file data to the FSx File Gateway. Configure cloud workloads to use FSx for Windows File Server and on-premises workloads to use the FSx File Gateway.
questions.explanation: FSx for Windows File Server provides a managed, native Windows file system in AWS. FSx File Gateway allows on-premises access with low latency while centralizing storage in FSx.

questions.prompt: A hospital recently deployed a RESTful API with Amazon API Gateway and AWS Lambda. The hospital uses API Gateway and Lambda to upload reports that are in PDF format and JPEG format. The hospital needs to modify the Lambda code to identify protected health information (PHI) in the reports. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?
questions.correctAnswer: Use Amazon Textract to extract text from the reports and Amazon Comprehend Medical to identify PHI from the extracted text.
questions.explanation: Textract extracts text from PDFs and images; Comprehend Medical can identify PHI entities, minimizing custom development.

questions.prompt: A company has an application that generates a large number of files, each approximately 5 MB in size. The files are stored in Amazon S3. Company policy requires the files to be stored for 4 years before they can be deleted. Immediate accessibility is always required as the files contain critical business data that is not easy to reproduce. The files are frequently accessed in the first 30 days of the object creation but are rarely accessed after the first 30 days. Which storage solution is MOST cost-effective?
questions.correctAnswer: Create an S3 lifecycle policy to move files from S3 Standard to S3 Standard-Infrequent Access 30 days from object creation, and delete the files 4 years after object creation.
questions.explanation: Transitioning to S3 Standard-IA after 30 days reduces storage costs while keeping data immediately accessible; lifecycle deletion enforces the 4-year retention requirement.
